en: Workplace hazard identification and assessment covering design, equipment, materials, processes, and training must be performed.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 4(1), 5

Hazard assessments must include documented preventive measures based on general prevention principles. Controls must address normal operations, emergency situations, and potential disruptions and be integrated at all organizational levels.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 4(1,3), 5, 7
Collective protection measures must be prioritized over individual protection.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 7(8)

Hazard assessments and preventive measures must be reviewed and updated as needed, including after accidents, occupational illnesses, psychosocial strain, changes in equipment or processes, or at the request of the authorities.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 4(4,5)
